qa.a: UBR (Ultimate Broker Routing) is a multi‑day routing/optimization tool for brokers and field agents. You feed it stops, constraints, and preferences — it outputs a practical route plan you can actually execute.

qa.q: How do I use UBR in 30 seconds?
qa.a: Add stops → set start location + day rules (max stops/day, day start/end) → run Optimize/Build → choose a route from the scoreboard → export/save.

qa.q: Why are my routes different each time?
qa.a: If the optimizer includes randomness (seed) or ties are broken by iteration order, results can vary. Try locking a seed or sorting stops deterministically before optimization.

qa.q: What should I check first if optimization looks wrong?
qa.a: Verify the cost function and constraints: distance vs time, penalties for time windows, max stops/day, and how day breaks are enforced. Validate with a small 5–10 stop test case.
If the app is fully static and relies on local storage, core UI can work offline — but map tiles/geocoding often need network unless cached or replaced with a no‑map mode.
